addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

3	description: "FollowPath in zopfli.c verifies but 0/52 kills — ~22 is_fresh objects exceed CBMC depth 200, body unreachable"
4	metadata:

6	  type: project

8	---
9	
10	`FollowPath` (static, zopfli.c ~line 3132) verifies with `run-cbmc` but scores 0/52 mutation kills. Same depth-bound vacuity as [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]] and [[cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity]].
11	
12	It needs ~22 `is_fresh` objects to satisfy its callees' memory-safety preconditions: `s`, `s->lmc` + cache arrays `length`/`dist`/`sublen`, hash `h` + its 8 arrays (head/prev/hashval/head2/prev2/hashval2/same), `store` + 8 arrays, `in`, and `path`. That far exceeds the ~17 in `ZopfliFindLongestMatch` (which it calls and which itself verifies vacuously). The is_fresh+malloc harness exhausts `--depth 200`, so the loop body is never reached → all 52 mutants survive.
13	
14	No non-vacuous middle ground: every is_fresh is required for soundness (drop any and a callee precondition / OOB check fails). Also note loop-carried contract-replacement issues would block non-vacuous verification anyway: UpdateHash havocs the whole head[] table (object_whole assigns) so the next UpdateHash's "all head in [-1,WINDOW)" precondition can't be re-established; StoreLitLenDist requires store->size==3 each call but size grows; FindLongestMatch is called with sublen=NULL (its contract requires is_fresh(sublen,...)). Vacuity masks all of these.
15	
16	Gotcha fixed during specification: the `__CPROVER_forall { path[kp] <= 258 }` precondition gave a real OOB FAILURE until `pathsize` was bounded (`pathsize >= 1 && pathsize <= inend - instart`) — unbounded pathsize lets `pathsize*sizeof` overflow and under-allocate the is_fresh object. Strong sound spec left in place.
17

---
name: cbmc-followpath-vacuity
description: "FollowPath in zopfli.c verifies but 0/52 kills — ~22 is_fresh objects exceed CBMC depth 200, body unreachable"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: ca2b2b17-60e7-4837-9649-eaf4d6541b55
---

`FollowPath` (static, zopfli.c ~line 3132) verifies with `run-cbmc` but scores 0/52 mutation kills. Same depth-bound vacuity as [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]] and [[cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity]].

It needs ~22 `is_fresh` objects to satisfy its callees' memory-safety preconditions: `s`, `s->lmc` + cache arrays `length`/`dist`/`sublen`, hash `h` + its 8 arrays (head/prev/hashval/head2/prev2/hashval2/same), `store` + 8 arrays, `in`, and `path`. That far exceeds the ~17 in `ZopfliFindLongestMatch` (which it calls and which itself verifies vacuously). The is_fresh+malloc harness exhausts `--depth 200`, so the loop body is never reached → all 52 mutants survive.

No non-vacuous middle ground: every is_fresh is required for soundness (drop any and a callee precondition / OOB check fails). Also note loop-carried contract-replacement issues would block non-vacuous verification anyway: UpdateHash havocs the whole head[] table (object_whole assigns) so the next UpdateHash's "all head in [-1,WINDOW)" precondition can't be re-established; StoreLitLenDist requires store->size==3 each call but size grows; FindLongestMatch is called with sublen=NULL (its contract requires is_fresh(sublen,...)). Vacuity masks all of these.

Gotcha fixed during specification: the `__CPROVER_forall { path[kp] <= 258 }` precondition gave a real OOB FAILURE until `pathsize` was bounded (`pathsize >= 1 && pathsize <= inend - instart`) — unbounded pathsize lets `pathsize*sizeof` overflow and under-allocate the is_fresh object. Strong sound spec left in place.

2	name: cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash
3	description: zopfli.c functions that call malloc() directly in their body cannot be verified — goto-instrument crashes under --enforce-contract
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: fb9c9a17-02a2-4e4c-870b-ee03d8bf9757
8	---
9	
10	`ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`, `ZopfliLengthsToSymbols` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — body mallocs `bl_count`/`next_code`), `OptimizeHuffmanForRle` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — body mallocs/frees `good_for_rle`), `ZopfliCleanCache` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — free-only body: `free(lmc->length/dist/sublen)`; crashes on the `__CPROVER_deallocate::...return_value___VERIFIER_nondet___CPROVER_bool` lvalue rather than malloc's `should_malloc_fail`, same root cause), `ZopfliCleanLZ77Store` (confirmed 2026-06-26 — free-only body freeing 7 store pointers; same `__CPROVER_deallocate` crash; crashes with OR without an `assigns`/`frees` clause), and any `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` function whose **body calls `malloc`/`free`/`realloc` directly** cannot be verified by `run-cbmc`. goto-instrument aborts BEFORE cbmc runs, with:
11	
12	```
13	instrument_spec_assigns.cpp:597 function: create_car_expr
14	Condition: size.has_value()
15	Reason: no definite size for lvalue target: malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail  (type bool, <builtin-library-malloc>)
16	```
17	
18	Root cause (proven by elimination, not clause-specific): `run-cbmc` always does `goto-instrument --enforce-contract <fn>` plus `--add-library` (which links CBMC's malloc model). When the function body calls malloc, the model's internal `should_malloc_fail` bool write gets caught by the frame-condition / car (conditional-address-range) instrumentation, which can't size it → invariant violation → `Aborted`. It is INDEPENDENT of contract content:
19	- removed caller `assigns` → crash; removed ALL `assigns` in file → crash
20	- callee memory-predicates (`is_fresh`→`rw_ok`/`1`) neutralized → crash
21	- callees given trivial `requires(1)/ensures(1)` → crash
22	- caller `is_fresh` requires removed → crash
23	
24	So NO specification on the function can avoid it, and you can't remove the malloc calls (no C edits). goto-cc still ACCEPTS the contract (compile passes); the abort is purely in the goto-instrument enforce step. Contrast: the documented is_fresh/depth-200 vacuity wall ([[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]]) hits functions that take is_fresh pointer params but DON'T call malloc themselves — those at least reach cbmc.
25	
26	Deliverable for `ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`: left a sound, strong contract anyway (requires `n>=0`, `1<=maxbits<=15`, is_fresh on `frequencies`/`bitlengths` of `n` elems; assigns object_whole(bitlengths); ensures return ∈{0,1} and `forall k<n: bitlengths[k] <= maxbits`). It is correct/sound but the tool can't check it. Callees `InitLists/BoundaryPM/BoundaryPMFinal/ExtractBitLengths` already have contracts. See [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]] (always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc`) and [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]].

---
name: cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash
description: zopfli.c functions that call malloc() directly in their body cannot be verified — goto-instrument crashes under --enforce-contract
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: fb9c9a17-02a2-4e4c-870b-ee03d8bf9757
---

`ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`, `ZopfliLengthsToSymbols` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — body mallocs `bl_count`/`next_code`), `OptimizeHuffmanForRle` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — body mallocs/frees `good_for_rle`), `ZopfliCleanCache` (confirmed 2026-06-25 — free-only body: `free(lmc->length/dist/sublen)`; crashes on the `__CPROVER_deallocate::...return_value___VERIFIER_nondet___CPROVER_bool` lvalue rather than malloc's `should_malloc_fail`, same root cause), `ZopfliCleanLZ77Store` (confirmed 2026-06-26 — free-only body freeing 7 store pointers; same `__CPROVER_deallocate` crash; crashes with OR without an `assigns`/`frees` clause), and any `Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` function whose **body calls `malloc`/`free`/`realloc` directly** cannot be verified by `run-cbmc`. goto-instrument aborts BEFORE cbmc runs, with:

```
instrument_spec_assigns.cpp:597 function: create_car_expr
Condition: size.has_value()
Reason: no definite size for lvalue target: malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail  (type bool, <builtin-library-malloc>)
```

Root cause (proven by elimination, not clause-specific): `run-cbmc` always does `goto-instrument --enforce-contract <fn>` plus `--add-library` (which links CBMC's malloc model). When the function body calls malloc, the model's internal `should_malloc_fail` bool write gets caught by the frame-condition / car (conditional-address-range) instrumentation, which can't size it → invariant violation → `Aborted`. It is INDEPENDENT of contract content:
- removed caller `assigns` → crash; removed ALL `assigns` in file → crash
- callee memory-predicates (`is_fresh`→`rw_ok`/`1`) neutralized → crash
- callees given trivial `requires(1)/ensures(1)` → crash
- caller `is_fresh` requires removed → crash

So NO specification on the function can avoid it, and you can't remove the malloc calls (no C edits). goto-cc still ACCEPTS the contract (compile passes); the abort is purely in the goto-instrument enforce step. Contrast: the documented is_fresh/depth-200 vacuity wall ([[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]]) hits functions that take is_fresh pointer params but DON'T call malloc themselves — those at least reach cbmc.

Deliverable for `ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`: left a sound, strong contract anyway (requires `n>=0`, `1<=maxbits<=15`, is_fresh on `frequencies`/`bitlengths` of `n` elems; assigns object_whole(bitlengths); ensures return ∈{0,1} and `forall k<n: bitlengths[k] <= maxbits`). It is correct/sound but the tool can't check it. Callees `InitLists/BoundaryPM/BoundaryPMFinal/ExtractBitLengths` already have contracts. See [[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]] (always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc`) and [[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]].

3133	                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
3134	                       unsigned short *path, size_t pathsize,
3135	                       ZopfliLZ77Store *store, ZopfliHash *h)
3136	/* Window of input. instart==inend triggers an early return, otherwise the loops
3137	   walk [windowstart, inend); require the buffer fresh for inend bytes. */
3138	__CPROVER_requires(instart <= inend)
3139	__CPROVER_requires(inend >= 1)
3140	__CPROVER_requires(inend < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3))
3141	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(in, inend))
3142	/* The path: pathsize lengths, each a valid match length (<= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH) so
3143	   that it is a legal limit argument to ZopfliFindLongestMatch. Each emitted
3144	   length is at least 1, so the path can hold at most inend - instart entries. */
3145	__CPROVER_requires(pathsize >= 1 && pathsize <= inend - instart)
3146	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(path, pathsize * sizeof(*path)))
3147	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
3148	    size_t kp; (kp < pathsize) ==> path[kp] <= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH
3149	})
3150	/* Block state and longest-match cache, as required by ZopfliFindLongestMatch.
3151	   pos walks [instart, inend); blockstart must not exceed instart so that
3152	   pos - blockstart is a valid (non-wrapping) cache index throughout. */
3153	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))
3154	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc, sizeof(*s->lmc)))
3155	__CPROVER_requires(s->blockstart <= instart)
3156	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->length, (inend - s->blockstart) * sizeof(unsigned short)))
3157	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->dist, (inend - s->blockstart) * sizeof(unsigned short)))
3158	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->sublen, ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * (inend - s->blockstart) * 3))
3159	/* Each cache slot that may be visited is unfilled (length==1, dist==0), the
3160	   precondition StoreInLongestMatchCache needs to store a fresh value. */
3161	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
3162	    size_t kc; (kc < inend - s->blockstart) ==> s->lmc->length[kc] == 1
3163	})
3164	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
3165	    size_t kd; (kd < inend - s->blockstart) ==> s->lmc->dist[kd] == 0
3166	})
3167	/* The hash structure: two hashes, each with head/prev/hashval arrays, plus the
3168	   shared "same" run-length array. */
3169	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3170	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
3171	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
3172	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(int)))
3173	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
3174	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
3175	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(int)))
3176	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
3177	/* The output store, configured as ZopfliStoreLitLenDist requires for an append
3178	   at index 3: size==3, the small arrays fresh for 4 elements, and the cumulative
3179	   histograms cleared so the first increment is observable. */

static void FollowPath(ZopfliBlockState *s,
                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
                       unsigned short *path, size_t pathsize,
                       ZopfliLZ77Store *store, ZopfliHash *h)
/* Window of input. instart==inend triggers an early return, otherwise the loops
   walk [windowstart, inend); require the buffer fresh for inend bytes. */
__CPROVER_requires(instart <= inend)
__CPROVER_requires(inend >= 1)
__CPROVER_requires(inend < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(in, inend))
/* The path: pathsize lengths, each a valid match length (<= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH) so
   that it is a legal limit argument to ZopfliFindLongestMatch. Each emitted
   length is at least 1, so the path can hold at most inend - instart entries. */
__CPROVER_requires(pathsize >= 1 && pathsize <= inend - instart)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(path, pathsize * sizeof(*path)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
    size_t kp; (kp < pathsize) ==> path[kp] <= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH
})
/* Block state and longest-match cache, as required by ZopfliFindLongestMatch.
   pos walks [instart, inend); blockstart must not exceed instart so that
   pos - blockstart is a valid (non-wrapping) cache index throughout. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))

static void TraceBackwards(size_t size, const unsigned short *length_array,
                           unsigned short **path, size_t *pathsize)
/* length_array holds the optimal length to reach each byte; it is indexed from 1
   up to and including `size`, so it must be fresh for size + 1 elements. Bound
   size so (size + 1) * sizeof never overflows the allocation model. */
__CPROVER_requires(size >= 1)
__CPROVER_requires(size < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (2 * sizeof(unsigned short)) - 1)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(length_array, (size + 1) * sizeof(*length_array)))
/* Each visited entry is a valid match length: at least 1 (so the walk makes
   progress and index reaches 0), at most ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH, and never larger than
   its own index (so index -= length_array[index] stays non-negative). These are
   exactly the three in-body asserts. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
    size_t k; (k >= 1 && k <= size) ==>
        (length_array[k] >= 1 && length_array[k] <= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH &&
         length_array[k] <= k)
})
/* The output path is grown from empty via ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA: *pathsize == 0 so
   the macro mallocs a fresh buffer; the *path slot and the size counter must be
   writable. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(path, sizeof(*path)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pathsize, sizeof(*pathsize)))
__CPROVER_requires(*pathsize == 0)
__CPROVER_assigns(*path, *pathsize)
/* Each iteration appends exactly one length and decreases index by at least 1
   from size down to 0, so the path holds between 1 and `size` entries. */
__CPROVER_ensures(*pathsize >= 1 && *pathsize <= size)

Done. `TraceBackwards` in `/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` now has a strong, sound CBMC contract and **verifies successfully**.

## Result
- **Verification:** ✅ `TraceBackwards verified successfully`
- **Mutation kill score:** 0/23 (vacuous — see below)

## Spec written
- `requires size >= 1` and a `max_malloc_size` bound so `(size+1)*sizeof` can't overflow.
- `is_fresh(length_array, (size+1) * sizeof(...))` — it's indexed up to and including `size` (consistent with `GetBestLengths`, which allocates `inend-instart+1`).
- A `__CPROVER_forall` constraining every visited entry to `[1, ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH]` and `<= k` — exactly discharging the three in-body asserts (`length_array[index] <= index`, `<= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH`, `!= 0`).
- `is_fresh(path)`, `is_fresh(pathsize)`, `*pathsize == 0` (matches the caller `LZ77OptimalRun`, which sets `*path=0; *pathsize=0` before calling), `assigns(*path, *pathsize)`, and `ensures 1 <= *pathsize <= size`.

## Why 0/23 is unavoidable
The score is 0 because verification is **vacuous** — I confirmed the body is unreachable (a deliberately-false `*pathsize == 999999` ensures still "Verified", even at `size == 2`). The cause is the `--depth 200` cap: `TraceBackwards` grows `path` via `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA`, whose macro expands to inline `malloc`/`realloc`/`memset`. CBMC's realloc/memset byte-copy models exhaust the depth budget before the path reaches the postcondition, so all 23 mutants survive. I can't edit the C (it's a macro) or change `--depth` (hardcoding rule), so this is a hard wall.

Notably, this **corrects** a prior note: I expected the in-body malloc to *crash* goto-instrument under `--enforce-contract` (as free-only/bare-malloc bodies do), but the `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` form is accepted and reaches CBMC — it just verifies vacuously. I've recorded this finding in memory.
